steps: To purify GST-FAM134C, fuse the cytosol-exposed domain of FAM134C (250-466aa) to a N-terminal synthesize the gene GST-tag by Genscript and clone into a pGEX-4T1 vector. Plasmid is also available from Addgene.

After the transformation of the pGEX-4T1 vector encoding GST-FAM134C in E. coli Rosetta pLysS cells (Novagen Cat# 70956-4), grow cells in 2x Tryptone Yeast extract (TY) medium at 37 °C until an OD 600 of 0.4 and then continue at 18 °C .
Elute the proteins with SEC buffer. SEC Buffer: %s %s

Pool the supernatant fractions, filter through a 0.45 µm syringe filter, concentrate with 30 kDa cut-off Amicon filter (Merck Millipore), and load onto a pre-equilibrated Superdex 200 Increase 10/300 GL column (Cytiva).
To collect the supernatant, collect the beads by centrifugation. Wash the beads twice with 4 mL of wash buffer, and collect the supernatant.
Incubate the beads Overnight with 4 mL of 50 millimolar (mM) reduced glutathione dissolved in wash buffer at 4 °C , to elute GST-FAM134C from the beads. Wash Buffer: %s %s

Centrifuge samples to pellet the beads and remove the unbound lysate. Then wash the beads twice with wash buffer, once with high salt wash buffer, and two more times with wash buffer. Wash buffer: %s %s Salt wash Buffer: %s %s
Sonicate the cell lysates twice for 00:00:30 and clear by centrifugation at 18000 rpm, 4 °C, 45 minutes in a SORVAL RC6+ centrifuge with an F21S-8x50Y rotor (Thermo Scientific).
Collect the cells by centrifugation and resuspend in lysis buffer. Lysis Buffer: %s %s
Once the cells reach an OD 600 of 0.8, induce the protein expression with 100 micromolar (µM) isopropyl β-D-1-thiogalactopyranoside (IPTG) for 16:00:00 at 18 °C .
Collect the supernatant and incubate with pre-equilibrated Glutathione Sepharose 4B beads (GE Healthcare) for 02:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle shaking to bind GST-FAM134C.
Analyze the fractions by SDS-PAGE and Coomassie staining. Pool the fractions containing purified GST-FAM134C.
After concentrating the purified protein, aliquot the protein and snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen.
creator: Elias Adriaenssens
